title: oxalis spiralis subsp. spiralis
intro: Oxalis spiralis subsp. spiralis, also known as the 'twisted wood sorrel', is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of oxalic acid. The health benefits of Oxalis spiralis subsp. spiralis include its potential in treating digestive issues, urinary tract infections, and certain skin conditions, as well as its role as a natural diuretic and blood purifier. Oxalis spiralis subsp. spiralis can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers are often used in herbal remedies, although it is essential to note that excessive consumption may lead to kidney damage due to its high oxalic acid content.
